zh.lifeSummary: Mrs Elizabeth Saint Barbe於1512年出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特阿生頓。她於大約1550年與William Saint Barbe Privey Chamber to Henry VIII在英國維爾爾結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs Elizabeth Saint Barbe于1512年出生在英国英格兰薩默塞特阿生頓。她于大约1550年与William Saint Barbe Privey Chamber to Henry VIII在英国維爾爾结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Luc, Camille, Lucien.  Spanish Alejandro, Anselmo, Carlos, Emilio, Jesusa, Jorge.
French (also Santé): probably a nickname for a healthy person, from French santé ‘good health’.

Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Spanish (southwestern): habitational name from one of several places called Sante, in Galicia, Portugal, and Asturias, from a Visigothic personal name. This surname is also found in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Barbe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Laurent, Andre, Gillis, Jean Pierre, Serge, Sylvie, Thierry, Yves.
French: descriptive nickname for someone with a beard, Old French barbe (from Latin barba).
French (Barbé): adjectival derivative of 1, meaning ‘bearded’.

variants.name: Barb
meanings: German: variant of   Barbe  . This surname is also found in France (mainly Alsace).
Romanian: variant of   Barbu  .

variants.name: Barbier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Dominique, Emile, Pierre, Alexandre, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Laurette, Manon, Marcel, Marcellin, Michel.
French: occupational name for a barber-surgeon (see Barber ), Old French barbier (from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’). Compare Barbe and Barbee .
German: occupational name for a barber, from Middle High German barbierer, or an occupational name for an armorer, from barbier(e) ‘metal visor (to protect the face)’.

variants.name: Barbon
meanings: English: habitational name from Barbon in Westmorland. The placename is probably from Old Norse bjórr ‘beaver’ + brunnr ‘stream’, alternatively from Old English bera ‘bear’ + burna ‘stream’.
English: nickname from Middle English bare bon ‘bare bone’, perhaps denoting a thin or bare-legged man.
French and English (of French Huguenot origin): nickname for a bearded man, from an augmentative of French   Barbe  .

variants.name: Barby
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Merwin, Otto, Wolfgang.  French Celestine, Pierre.
German: habitational name from a place so named in Saxony-Anhalt.
French (mainly Dordogne): derivative of   Barbe   1.

variants.name: Barbin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Angelle, Marcel.
French: diminutive of   Barbe   1.

variants.name: Barbot
meanings: French:
from the Old French female personal name Barbot, a diminutive of Barbe , a short form of Barbara .
nickname for someone who tended to speak indistinctly, from French barboter ‘to babble’, or for someone with a beard, from a diminutive of Old French barbe ‘beard’.

variants.name: Barbay
meanings: French: variant of Barbé (see Barbe ).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Elizabeth Saint Barbe was born in 1512, in Ashington, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. She married William Saint Barbe Privey Chamber to Henry VIII about 1550, in Wiltshire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs Elizabeth Saint Barbe est née en 1512, à Ashington, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé William Saint Barbe Privey Chamber to Henry VIII environ 1550, à Wiltshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs Elizabeth Saint Barbe wurde 1512 in Ashington, Somerset, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat um 1550 in Wiltshire, England William Saint Barbe Privey Chamber to Henry VIII geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs Elizabeth Saint Barbe è nata il nel 1512 nel Ashington, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei sposato William Saint Barbe Privey Chamber to Henry VIII circa 1550, nel Wiltshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Mrs Elizabeth Saint Barbeは1512年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトアシュイントンで生まれた。約1550年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアでWilliam Saint Barbe Privey Chamber to Henry VIIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs Elizabeth Saint Barbe(은)는 1512년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 애신그턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1550년 영국 윌츠히르에서 William Saint Barbe Privey Chamber to Henry VIII(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs Elizabeth Saint Barbe nasceu em 1512, em Ashington, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela casou-se com William Saint Barbe Privey Chamber to Henry VIII aproximadamente 1550, em Wiltshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs Elizabeth Saint Barbe родилась в 1512 г. в местности ашингтон, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Saint Barbe Privey Chamber to Henry VIII около 1550 г. в местности уилцхир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs Elizabeth Saint Barbe nació en 1512, en Ashington, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Se casó con William Saint Barbe Privey Chamber to Henry VIII aproximadamente 1550, en Wiltshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 1 hija.
